title: Transfer Lab: Repair an Emergency Resource Directory
summary: Transfer Lab: Repair an Emergency Resource Directory retrieves prior skills in an original unfamiliar token language URL and ARIA-state selector incident scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply css attribute presence value, css attribute token language, css attribute substrings, css data aria state in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real unfamiliar token language URL and ARIA-state selector incident artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.
